summary: Full backend + frontend testing of FixForge DIY aggregator. All 19 pytest cases passed (100%): auth gating, project search via Claude (LLM working), free preview gating (locked=true, 1 step, resources=[], safety_tips=[]), Pro unlock (full 10 steps, resources populated), favorites CRUD with auth, collections CRUD with auth, Stripe payment session creation for both 'guide' ($2.99) and 'pro' ($9.00) packages, server-side amount enforcement (client-spoofed amount=0.01 ignored, txn records $2.99/$9.00), checkout status endpoint returns unpaid as expected. Frontend: landing page loads, search inputs present, project detail page shows paywall with unlock + go-pro buttons + tools/materials always visible + print buttons; authenticated dashboard shows favorites/collections tabs; Pro session yields no paywall and full steps.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 562-line file mixing auth/projects/favorites/collections/payments - consider splitting into routers (routers/auth.py, routers/projects.py, routers/payments.py) before it grows further.
PACKAGES amount uses python float - acceptable for current Stripe integration which receives Decimal-safe values, but document this to avoid future drift.
checkout_status auto-triggers grant_access on poll - good idempotency via 'processed' flag. Webhook also handles it; both are safe.

Console shows two 401s on initial unauthenticated page load (auth/me + favorites/ids). They are silently handled in the UI but could be suppressed with a single-fire 'check auth then conditionally fetch favorites' pattern to keep console clean.
extract_json() is permissive but does not handle nested triple-backtick fences with language hints beyond 'json'. Acceptable for current Claude output.
test_credentials: Seeded via mongosh - free user token: test_session_1782475351046 (user_id test-user-1782475351046), pro user token: test_session_pro_1782475351057 (user_id test-pro-1782475351057, is_pro=true). Existing test project: proj_22f1086ecb88 (Wall-Mounted Wooden Spice Rack, 10 steps).
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test users (free + pro) and 2 sessions in db.users / db.user_sessions per /app/auth_testing.md. Pre-existing seed proj_22f1086ecb88 used for gating tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_fixforge_api.py is reusable. It depends on two seeded tokens (free + pro) and the existing project proj_22f1086ecb88; if tokens get cleaned up, regenerate via mongosh snippet in /app/memory/test_credentials.md and update TEST_TOKEN_FREE / TEST_TOKEN_PRO env vars or constants in the test file. Stripe is in test mode (sk_test_emergent); never attempt to complete a real payment - just verify session creation + status + transaction amount.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Server-side pricing was verified directly in db.payment_transactions: client sending amount=0.01 was correctly ignored and PACKAGES['guide']=2.99 / PACKAGES['pro']=9.0 were persisted. Free preview gating returns locked=true with steps[:1], resources=[], safety_tips=[] while keeping tools+materials populated (shopping list free for everyone). Pro user (is_pro=true) bypasses gating and gets full payload.
